content.text:
Can you check what are the pending items for Week 7, and create a plan for the implementation. You can use the harness data and playwirght for the testing framework.

And we need to enrich the git page with what are features dataq offers, what are different modes, how to add a test case / suit / connection, how to run suit schedule / trigger bind, notification channels, troubleshooting, and best practices for using DataQ effectively, etc. 

Also can you find out list of potencial API endpoints taht can be exposed to MCP.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

**Total: 73 FastAPI endpoints**

---

### (B) THE 8 CURRENT MCP TOOLS

Exposed in `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/mcp/server.py`:

| Tool Name | Input | Output Type | Service Wrapper | Authz Pattern | Read/Mutating |
|-----------|-------|-------------|-----------------|----------------|---------------|
| **list_suites()** | none | `list[dict]` per-suite: id, name, datasource, env, check_count, last_run | `suite_service.list_suites(session, user_id)` | Per-suite: scoped to owned/shared via `suite_service.list_suites` + `accessible_suite_ids(user.id)` | **Read** |
| **get_suite_results(suite_id)** | suite_id (str UUID) | `dict`: suite_id, run{id,status,started_at,finished_at}, checks[{name,status,metric_value,observed_value,expected_value,sample_failures}] | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "view")` → `run_service.list_results(run_id)` + `run_service.redact_sample_failures(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="view")` + sample redaction per ADR 0008 (#226/#415) | **Read** |
| **get_health_score(window_days)** | window_days (int, default 7, max 90) | `dict`: window_days, health_score, pass_rate, total_runs, active_connections, trend[{day,succeeded,failed}] | `dashboard_service.dashboard_summary(session, user_id, window_days)` | Per-suite: scoped to accessible suites via `dashboard_service` user_id filter | **Read** |
| **get_adf_pipeline_status(provider, limit)** | provider (str: "adf"/"airflow", optional), limit (int, default 20) | `list[dict]`: provider, pipeline, status, started_at, finished_at, dq_run{run_id,status} | `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs(session, provider, limit)` + correlate to DQ runs via `accessible_suite_ids(user.id)` filter | Authenticated-only (orchestration monitoring, not suite-scoped); DQ-run correlation checks suite accessibility | **Read** |
| **trigger_suite_run(suite_id)** | suite_id (str UUID) | `dict`: run_id, status="queued" | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → create `Run` → `run_dispatch.dispatch_or_fail(run)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Mutating** |
| **get_run_status(run_id)** | run_id (str UUID) | `dict`: run_id, status, total_checks, completed_checks, counts{}, checks[{name,status}] | `run_service.get_run(run_id)` + `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "view")` + `run_service.get_run_progress(run)` | Per-suite: gates on the run's suite via `require_permission(..., minimum="view")` (404 hides run if suite inaccessible) | **Read** |
| **create_check(suite_id, name, expectation_type, config, kind, warn/fail/critical_threshold)** | suite_id, name, expectation_type, config dict, thresholds | `dict`: id, suite_id, name, expectation_type | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → `check_service.create_check(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Mutating** |
| **profile_column(suite_id, columns, table/schema/catalog/path/file_format, top_n)** | suite_id, columns[], SQL target or file path, top_n (default 10) | `dict`: row_count, table/schema/catalog (SQL) or path/file_format (file), columns[{column,null_count,null_fraction,distinct_count,min_value,max_value,top_values}] | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → get connection → `profile_service.profile_connection(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Read** (no persistence) |

**MCP Tools Summary:**
- **4 read tools** (list_suites, get_suite_results, get_health_score, get_adf_pipeline_status)
- **2 read/preview tools** (get_run_status, profile_column — read data, no persistence)
- **2 action tools** (trigger_suite_run, create_check — mutate state)
- **All 8 as MCP tools, not resources** (per ADR 0008 — LLM invocability)
- **Zero logic duplication** — each wraps an existing service function with the same authz as REST routers

---

### (C) SERVICE-LAYER REUSE & AUTHZ PATTERN

**Services reused by MCP tools:**
- `suite_service.list_suites()` — read-scoped by user_id
- `run_service.list_results()`, `run_service.redact_sample_failures()` — sample redaction per #415
- `run_service.get_run()`, `run_service.get_run_progress()`
- `dashboard_service.dashboard_summary()` — scoped to user's accessible suites
- `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs()`
- `check_service.create_check()` — check creation + versioning
- `profile_service.profile_connection()` — column profiling

**Authz pattern (ADR 0008 compliance):**
1. **Per-suite authz via `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, minimum="view"|"edit"|"admin")`** — identical to REST routers
2. **Existence-hiding** — on 404 from require_permission, return a clean ToolError (not an opaque masked exception)
3. **Sample redaction (#415)** — `run_service.redact_sample_failures()` redacts PII from failing-row samples column-aware: tested_column + suite.column_policy drive which values surface
4. **Workspace-admin bypass** — implicit in `suite_service.list_suites(..., include_all=True)` and `dashboard_service` — admin sees all suites

**Example (from server.py line 159-191):**
```python
suite = require_permission(session, sid, user.id, minimum="view")
latest = session.scalars(select(Run).where(Run.suite_id == sid)...).first()
results = run_service.list_results(session, latest.id)
return {
    "checks": [
        {
            "sample_failures": run_service.redact_sample_failures(
                r.sample_failures,
                tested_column=(checks[r.check_id].config.get("column") if r.check_id in checks else None),
                policy=policy,  # suite.column_policy
            ),
        }
        for r in results
    ],
}
```

---

### (D) ADR 0008 CRITERIA & DEFERRED CANDIDATES

**Stated criteria (lines 16-26):**
1. **All 8 are MCP tools, not resources** — "Claude answers the canonical NL queries" (what failed today? / run the orders suite on DEV / why did the customer pipeline fail? / add a null check on email)
2. **Each tool is a thin wrapper** — open session → resolve caller → call same service + same `require_permission` authz → return LLM-shaped dict
3. **Sample redaction inherited from REST** — `run_service.redact_sample_failures` (same path as web UI)
4. **Per-suite sharing, existence-hiding, redaction for free** — one authz + redaction implementation

**Explicitly-deferred candidates (ADR 0008, Alternatives Considered):**
- **Resources for the reads** (lines 39) — rejected: "revisit if a client surfaces resources usefully"
- Implies: if fastmcp resource-templates become auto-callable in future clients, consider demoting `list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`, `get_adf_pipeline_status` to resources

**No explicit list of "future tools"** — the ADR covers the accepted 8; expansion is future work outside this ADR's scope.

---

### (E) ENDPOINTS NOT COVERED BY MCP TOOLS

**REST endpoints with NO MCP tool equivalent:**

**Suites (6 missing):**
- `POST /suites/import` — import suite document
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/export` — export suite document
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/columns` — list columns (column picker helper)
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/column-policy/suggest` — suggest redaction policy

**Checks (6 missing):**
- `DELETE /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}` — delete check
- `PATCH /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}` — update check
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/snooze` — snooze alerts
- `DELETE /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/snooze` — clear snooze
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/versions` — check history
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/history` — result trend history
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/checks/dryrun` — dry-run a check

**Connections (8 missing):**
- All 8 connection endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete, test, reauth, version history)

**Runs (2 missing):**
- `POST /runs/{run_id}/cancel` — cancel a run
- `GET /orchestration/pipelines` — list pipelines (vs. pipeline runs)

**Orchestration (2 missing):**
- Both webhook receiver endpoints (ADF + Airflow) — machine-to-machine, not user-facing

**Schedules (5 missing):**
- All 5 schedule endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete)

**Notifications (3 missing):**
- All 3 notification endpoints (get, put, delete)

**Shares (4 missing):**
- All 4 share endpoints (grant, list, update, revoke)

**Dashboard (0 missing):**
- ✓ Dashboard summary is **partially covered** by `get_health_score()` (KPIs + trend, but not suite_performance)

**Admin (4 missing):**
- All 4 admin endpoints (all suites, all users, access overview, webhook config)

**Auth (2 missing):**
- `GET /me` — current user profile
- `GET /users/search` — user directory search

**Trigger Bindings (5 missing):**
- All 5 trigger-binding endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete)

**Probe (2 missing):**
- Both probe endpoints (trigger + read) — demonstrator only

**Total uncovered: ~52 endpoints**

The MCP surface intentionally focuses on the **core LLM queries** (ADR 0008): DQ monitoring & alerts (8 tools), omitting suite/connection/user management (REST-only administrative tasks) and machine-to-machine orchestration webhooks.</result>

#### **USERS** (`backend/app/api/v1/users.py`)

| Method | Path | Purpose |

#### **PROBE** (`backend/app/api/v1/probe.py` — demonstrator)
| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|

| GET | `/_probe/runs/{run_id}` | Read a probe run and its results |

**Total: 73 FastAPI endpoints**

---

### (B) THE 8 CURRENT MCP TOOLS

Exposed in `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/mcp/server.py`:

| Tool Name | Input | Output Type | Service Wrapper | Authz Pattern | Read/Mutating |
|-----------|-------|-------------|-----------------|----------------|---------------|
| **list_suites()** | none | `list[dict]` per-suite: id, name, datasource, env, check_count, last_run | `suite_service.list_suites(session, user_id)` | Per-suite: scoped to owned/shared via `suite_service.list_suites` + `accessible_suite_ids(user.id)` | **Read** |
| **get_suite_results(suite_id)** | suite_id (str UUID) | `dict`: suite_id, run{id,status,started_at,finished_at}, checks[{name,status,metric_value,observed_value,expected_value,sample_failures}] | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "view")` → `run_service.list_results(run_id)` + `run_service.redact_sample_failures(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="view")` + sample redaction per ADR 0008 (#226/#415) | **Read** |
| **get_health_score(window_days)** | window_days (int, default 7, max 90) | `dict`: window_days, health_score, pass_rate, total_runs, active_connections, trend[{day,succeeded,failed}] | `dashboard_service.dashboard_summary(session, user_id, window_days)` | Per-suite: scoped to accessible suites via `dashboard_service` user_id filter | **Read** |
| **get_adf_pipeline_status(provider, limit)** | provider (str: "adf"/"airflow", optional), limit (int, default 20) | `list[dict]`: provider, pipeline, status, started_at, finished_at, dq_run{run_id,status} | `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs(session, provider, limit)` + correlate to DQ runs via `accessible_suite_ids(user.id)` filter | Authenticated-only (orchestration monitoring, not suite-scoped); DQ-run correlation checks suite accessibility | **Read** |
| **trigger_suite_run(suite_id)** | suite_id (str UUID) | `dict`: run_id, status="queued" | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → create `Run` → `run_dispatch.dispatch_or_fail(run)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Mutating** |
| **get_run_status(run_id)** | run_id (str UUID) | `dict`: run_id, status, total_checks, completed_checks, counts{}, checks[{name,status}] | `run_service.get_run(run_id)` + `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "view")` + `run_service.get_run_progress(run)` | Per-suite: gates on the run's suite via `require_permission(..., minimum="view")` (404 hides run if suite inaccessible) | **Read** |
| **create_check(suite_id, name, expectation_type, config, kind, warn/fail/critical_threshold)** | suite_id, name, expectation_type, config dict, thresholds | `dict`: id, suite_id, name, expectation_type | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → `check_service.create_check(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Mutating** |
| **profile_column(suite_id, columns, table/schema/catalog/path/file_format, top_n)** | suite_id, columns[], SQL target or file path, top_n (default 10) | `dict`: row_count, table/schema/catalog (SQL) or path/file_format (file), columns[{column,null_count,null_fraction,distinct_count,min_value,max_value,top_values}] | `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, "edit")` → get connection → `profile_service.profile_connection(...)` | Per-suite: `require_permission(..., minimum="edit")` | **Read** (no persistence) |

**MCP Tools Summary:**
- **4 read tools** (list_suites, get_suite_results, get_health_score, get_adf_pipeline_status)
- **2 read/preview tools** (get_run_status, profile_column — read data, no persistence)
- **2 action tools** (trigger_suite_run, create_check — mutate state)
- **All 8 as MCP tools, not resources** (per ADR 0008 — LLM invocability)
- **Zero logic duplication** — each wraps an existing service function with the same authz as REST routers

---

### (C) SERVICE-LAYER REUSE & AUTHZ PATTERN

**Services reused by MCP tools:**
- `suite_service.list_suites()` — read-scoped by user_id
- `run_service.list_results()`, `run_service.redact_sample_failures()` — sample redaction per #415
- `run_service.get_run()`, `run_service.get_run_progress()`
- `dashboard_service.dashboard_summary()` — scoped to user's accessible suites
- `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs()`
- `check_service.create_check()` — check creation + versioning
- `profile_service.profile_connection()` — column profiling

**Authz pattern (ADR 0008 compliance):**
1. **Per-suite authz via `require_permission(suite_id, user_id, minimum="view"|"edit"|"admin")`** — identical to REST routers
2. **Existence-hiding** — on 404 from require_permission, return a clean ToolError (not an opaque masked exception)
3. **Sample redaction (#415)** — `run_service.redact_sample_failures()` redacts PII from failing-row samples column-aware: tested_column + suite.column_policy drive which values surface
4. **Workspace-admin bypass** — implicit in `suite_service.list_suites(..., include_all=True)` and `dashboard_service` — admin sees all suites

**Example (from server.py line 159-191):**
```python
suite = require_permission(session, sid, user.id, minimum="view")
latest = session.scalars(select(Run).where(Run.suite_id == sid)...).first()
results = run_service.list_results(session, latest.id)
return {
    "checks": [
        {
            "sample_failures": run_service.redact_sample_failures(
                r.sample_failures,
                tested_column=(checks[r.check_id].config.get("column") if r.check_id in checks else None),
                policy=policy,  # suite.column_policy
            ),
        }
        for r in results
    ],
}
```

---

### (D) ADR 0008 CRITERIA & DEFERRED CANDIDATES

**Stated criteria (lines 16-26):**
1. **All 8 are MCP tools, not resources** — "Claude answers the canonical NL queries" (what failed today? / run the orders suite on DEV / why did the customer pipeline fail? / add a null check on email)
2. **Each tool is a thin wrapper** — open session → resolve caller → call same service + same `require_permission` authz → return LLM-shaped dict
3. **Sample redaction inherited from REST** — `run_service.redact_sample_failures` (same path as web UI)
4. **Per-suite sharing, existence-hiding, redaction for free** — one authz + redaction implementation

**Explicitly-deferred candidates (ADR 0008, Alternatives Considered):**
- **Resources for the reads** (lines 39) — rejected: "revisit if a client surfaces resources usefully"
- Implies: if fastmcp resource-templates become auto-callable in future clients, consider demoting `list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`, `get_adf_pipeline_status` to resources

**No explicit list of "future tools"** — the ADR covers the accepted 8; expansion is future work outside this ADR's scope.

---

### (E) ENDPOINTS NOT COVERED BY MCP TOOLS

**REST endpoints with NO MCP tool equivalent:**

**Suites (6 missing):**
- `POST /suites/import` — import suite document
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/export` — export suite document
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/columns` — list columns (column picker helper)
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/column-policy/suggest` — suggest redaction policy

**Checks (6 missing):**
- `DELETE /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}` — delete check
- `PATCH /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}` — update check
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/snooze` — snooze alerts
- `DELETE /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/snooze` — clear snooze
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/versions` — check history
- `GET /suites/{suite_id}/checks/{check_id}/history` — result trend history
- `POST /suites/{suite_id}/checks/dryrun` — dry-run a check

**Connections (8 missing):**
- All 8 connection endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete, test, reauth, version history)

**Runs (2 missing):**
- `POST /runs/{run_id}/cancel` — cancel a run
- `GET /orchestration/pipelines` — list pipelines (vs. pipeline runs)

**Orchestration (2 missing):**
- Both webhook receiver endpoints (ADF + Airflow) — machine-to-machine, not user-facing

**Schedules (5 missing):**
- All 5 schedule endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete)

**Notifications (3 missing):**
- All 3 notification endpoints (get, put, delete)

**Shares (4 missing):**
- All 4 share endpoints (grant, list, update, revoke)

**Dashboard (0 missing):**
- ✓ Dashboard summary is **partially covered** by `get_health_score()` (KPIs + trend, but not suite_performance)

**Admin (4 missing):**
- All 4 admin endpoints (all suites, all users, access overview, webhook config)

**Auth (2 missing):**
- `GET /me` — current user profile
- `GET /users/search` — user directory search

**Trigger Bindings (5 missing):**
- All 5 trigger-binding endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete)

**Probe (2 missing):**
- Both probe endpoints (trigger + read) — demonstrator only

**Total uncovered: ~52 endpoints**

The MCP surface intentionally focuses on the **core LLM queries** (ADR 0008): DQ monitoring & alerts (8 tools), omitting suite/connection/user management (REST-only administrative tasks) and machine-to-machine orchestration webhooks.</result>

#### **Documented Topics vs. Gaps:**

| Topic | Covered? | Where | Notes |

- **CI integration:** `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — Postgres + Redis services, backend migrations, dev-seed, uvicorn on :8000, Playwright on :3000
- **Dev-bypass:** `VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true` allows testing without real Azure AD

**Backend Test Setup:**
- **pytest** — unit + integration tests
- **httpx** — TestClient for API endpoint testing
- **Structure:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/tests/`
  - `conftest.py` — transactional Postgres `db_session` fixture, fixtures for CheckRunner/mocks
  - Subdirs: `api/`, `services/`, `core/`, `datasources/`, `orchestration/`, `db/`, `e2e/`, `integration/`, `mcp/`, `alerting/`, `worker/`
  - **Full-stack API E2E smoke:** `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` (12/12 passing) — tests all six connection types, CRUD, severity thresholds, authoring loop (create→dry-run→delete)

**Coverage State:**
- **Backend:** ~91% (gates enforced in CI via mypy on `backend/tests`, coverage currently uncapped but moving to `--cov-fail-under=80%` in Week 8)
- **Frontend:** ~80% lines (Vitest)
- **Week 8 target:** ≥80% across both (CI-enforced)

---

### (c) SECURITY-HARDENING TERRAFORM TOGGLE STATE

**File:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/deploy/terraform/azure/variables.tf` (lines 90–96)

#### **Key Vault Purge Protection:**
```hcl
variable "key_vault_purge_protection" {
  description = "Key Vault purge protection. false during bring-up so a destroy/re-apply can reuse the vault name. PROD: set true to make secrets unrecoverable-deletable only after the soft-delete retention window (NOTE: irreversible once enabled)."
  type        = bool
  default     = false
}
```
- **Status:** ⬜ **NOT ENABLED** (default: `false`)
- **Wired in:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/deploy/terraform/azure/keyvault.tf` line 20 — `purge_protection_enabled = var.key_vault_purge_protection`
- **Action to enable:** Set `key_vault_purge_protection = true` in `terraform.tfvars` (gitignored) before prod apply — **irreversible once enabled**

#### **Postgres VNet-Private:**
- **Status:** ⬜ **NOT HARDENED** — current config uses shared Postgres Flexible Server (harness-owned) with **firewall rule `allow-Azure-services`** (allows any Azure service)
- **File:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/deploy/terraform/azure/postgres.tf` — reads the **shared** server via data source, no private VNet config in the app stack
- **Rationale:** Single-tenant v1 on a shared subscription; a VNET-isolated (shared or app-only) Postgres is a post-v1 hardening item (ADR 0010 seam-agnostic, no AWS/GCP equivalent yet)

**No other security toggles found.** The deploy is production-ready for the v1 single-tenant model (least-priv DB role, RBAC on Key Vault, UAMI auth, TLS-enforced Postgres, SPA-only frontend, Azure AD SSO).

---

### (d) HARNESS CAPABILITIES SUMMARY

**Location:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` (external, not git-tracked in main repo)

#### **Live Test Data Environments (Status: LIVE END-TO-END per ADR 0021)**

**Infrastructure (Terraform-deployed, 45 resources):**
- **Snowflake** (AWS, retail data warehouse) — 24 datasets, 18 deliberate DQ issues seeded
- **Databricks/Unity Catalog** (AWS, serverless SQL warehouse) — medallion reference / raw / silver / gold
- **ADLS Gen2** (Azure, landing zone) — flat-files (tracking, inventory)
- **ADF** (`dataq-harness-adf`) — event + timer trigger pipelines
- **Airflow** (Azure Container Apps) — scheduler + CeleryExecutor + 2 worker replicas
- **Redis** (broker for Celery)
- **Postgres Flexible Server** (westus3, Airflow metadata only)

#### **Orchestration Matrix (Both ADF + Airflow, both event + schedule):**

| | Event-driven | Timer / schedule |
|---|---|---|
| **ADF** | `pl_flow_a_orders` (blob-event) → Snowflake `ORDERS_HEADER` | `pl_flow_a_customers` (schedule) → Snowflake `CUSTOMERS` |
| **Airflow** | `flow_a_payments_event` (Event Grid push) → Snowflake `PAYMENTS` | `flow_a_snowflake_load` (9 feeds) + `flow_a_uc_reference` (8 dims) + `flow_b_medallion` (bronze→silver→gold) |

#### **Reference Flows (DataQ Runs Against):**

1. **Flow A — Snowflake** (`flow_a_snowflake_load` DAG + `pl_flow_a_orders` ADF)
   - Suite: `flow_a_snowflake` with column expectations + custom-SQL reconciliation check
   - Trigger-bindings: `(airflow, flow_a_snowflake_load, <env>)` + `(adf, pl_flow_a_orders, <env>)`

2. **Flow A — Unity Catalog reference** (`flow_a_uc_reference` DAG)
   - Suite: `flow_a_uc_reference` with not-null keys, allowed channel set, FK-ish checks
   - Trigger-binding: `(airflow, flow_a_uc_reference, <env>)`

3. **Flow B — Medallion (raw→silver→gold)** (`flow_b_medallion` DAG)
   - Suites: `flow_b_silver` (PII redaction: email hashed), `flow_b_gold` (freshness, volume, non-negative revenue)
   - Trigger-binding: `(airflow, flow_b_medallion, <env>)` → gold suite

4. **Flow C — Flat-file in place** (no orchestrator, DataQ runs on schedule)
   - Files: tracking (`*.csv`), inventory (`*.parquet`) — batch-resolved by DataQ
   - Suites: status lifecycle check, non-null tracking_number; inventory `on_hand >= 0`, freshness on `load_ts`

#### **Live Data Capabilities:**
- **Mock-data generators** — run on Container Apps cron (orders/tracking/inventory/feedback/supply → ADLS)
- **All 6 connection types tested:** Snowflake, ADLS, S3, Databricks/UC, ADF, Airflow
- **All 4 load modalities live:** ADF blob-event, ADF schedule, Airflow event-push (Event Grid), Airflow schedule
- **PII/PHI seeding:** Customers table has email/phone; medallion feedback email is hashed
- **18 deliberate DQ issues:** null values, duplicates, schema violations, freshness/volume outliers, reconciliation mismatches

#### **DataQ Setup (Adjacent Harness Config):**
- **Location:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/dataq_setup/`
- **Setup script:** `setup.py` — declarative, spec-driven (must match live OpenAPI)
- **Creates:** Connections (Snowflake, UC, ADLS), Suites (flow_a_snowflake, flow_a_uc_reference, flow_b_silver/gold), Checks (column expectations + custom-SQL), Trigger-bindings (exact match to DAG ids / ADF pipeline names)

**Status:** ✅ **Harness applied (45 resources), all flows running, DataQ verified runs in progress** (unblocked by the v1 cloud deploy in Week 7). The harness discharges the standing "live warehouse/file run smoke" caveat and feeds the team-onboarding demo seed.

Design a concrete, phased implementation plan for the DataQ repo at /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ. Read files only as needed to validate specifics — the exploration below is already done and trustworthy.

# Context (already explored — do not re-derive)

DataQ is a data-quality platform (FastAPI + Celery + Postgres backend, React/Vite/AntD frontend, GX Core engine), Week 7 of 8, deployed live to Azure Container Apps. Working agreements: trunk-based, squash-merge PRs, conventional commits, one functionality per commit, defects get GitHub issues, every functionality ships with tests, `Fixes #N` in PRs, format/lint/mypy/bandit/tests run locally before push. Docs are MkDocs Material published to GitHub Pages (https://theurgicduke771.github.io/DataQ/) via .github/workflows/docs.yml, strict build, nav in mkdocs.yml.

## Workstream 1 — Week 7 pending items (31/40 done). Open items:

In-repo actionable (GitHub issues):
- #92 — Admin UI should surface the ADF webhook URL (currently hand-assembled secret-bearing URL); there is already GET /admin/orchestration/webhooks
- #168 — Auth silent-token-refresh fallback (W1 carryover, was MSAL InteractionRequiredAuthError; frontend has since migrated to generic oidc-client-ts per ADR 0028 — needs re-scoping to oidc-client-ts silent-renew failure → interactive redirect)
- #17 — MCP server polish: CLAUDE.md section + supply-chain audit cadence
- #288 — modular user-friendly project documentation (overlaps workstream 2)
- #382 — deploy workflow: gate frontend deploy on backend success + pin image to SHA
- #396 — SWA linked-backend null_resource swallows errors — likely OBSOLETE: the SWA was destroyed in the ADR 0028 cutover (frontend now a Container App). Verify and close-as-obsolete.
- #397 — ci(deploy): migrate-gate poll burns full 15 min on unmatched job status

In-repo partial:
- App Insights span instrumentation (logs/exceptions live; request/task spans pending, OTel-neutral path preferred)
- Real-vault integration test (lazy-import branch of AzureKeyVaultStore at 0% coverage)
- KV purge-protection tfvars toggle exists (default false; enabling is irreversible — external decision)

External/live (coordination checklist, still plan them as verification steps where automatable):
- Azure Monitor→ADF webhook alert rule (5 roadmap tasks: action groups + alert rules + smoke)
- E2E live run paths Snowflake/flat-file/UC against the harness (see harness below)
- Claude Desktop E2E MCP smoke (4 canonical NL queries)
- Team onboarding session

## Testing state (user wants "harness data + Playwright for the testing framework")
- Playwright fully set up: frontend/playwright.config.ts, @playwright/test 1.61, frontend/e2e/ with 4 specs (smoke, connections, suites, results), 6 tests passing, CI job frontend-e2e (Postgres+Redis+backend+seed+uvicorn+vite), auth dev-bypass VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true.
- Gaps in E2E coverage: schedules UI, trigger-bindings UI, notification config UI, run-detail drill-down, dashboard widgets, check editor variants (custom SQL/freshness/volume), admin page.
- Live harness at /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/ (not git-tracked): 45 TF resources live — Snowflake retail warehouse (24 datasets, 18 seeded DQ issues), UC/Databricks medallion, ADLS flat files, ADF pipelines (event+schedule), Airflow DAGs (event+schedule), mock-data generators on cron. dataq_setup/setup.py declaratively creates connections/suites/checks/trigger-bindings against the live API (flow_a_snowflake, flow_a_uc_reference, flow_b_silver/gold, flow C flat-file schedule).
- Backend: pytest ~91% coverage; backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py 12/12.

## Workstream 2 — docs enrichment ("git page" = GitHub Pages MkDocs site + README), maps to issue #288. Existing docs/: index, getting-started, concepts, datasources-checks, orchestration, observability, architecture, runbook-faq, glossary. Gaps found:
- Notification channels (Teams/Slack/email setup, per-suite config, severity routing, dedup, snooze) — MISSING entirely
- Scheduling how-to (cron syntax, timezone/DST, no-backfill semantics) — minimal
- Best practices guide — MISSING
- Feature overview/matrix (check kinds × datasources, modes: expectation/custom-SQL/freshness/volume) — partial
- Per-check-type authoring walkthroughs — partial
- Troubleshooting depth — basic table exists in observability.md
Frontend routes for doc reference: /dashboard /connections(/new,/:id/edit) /suites(/new,/:id,/:id/edit,/:id/checks/new,/:id/checks/:id/edit) /results(/:runId) /profile /admin /settings.

## Workstream 3 — MCP candidate list (ANALYSIS ONLY, deliver a recommended list, don't implement):
Existing 8 tools: list_suites, get_suite_results, get_health_score, get_adf_pipeline_status, trigger_suite_run, create_check, get_run_status, profile_column. All thin wrappers over service layer with require_permission authz + sample redaction (ADR 0008). ~52 REST endpoints uncovered, including: checks CRUD/dryrun/history/snooze, connections list/test, runs list/cancel, schedules CRUD, trigger-bindings CRUD, notifications config, shares, dashboard suite-performance, orchestration/pipelines, suite export/import, column-policy suggest. Excluded by nature: webhooks (M2M), admin, probe, connection create/reauth (secrets through LLM = no).

# What I need from you

A step-by-step implementation plan organized as PR-sized phases, respecting the working agreements. For each phase: goal, files to touch (validate paths exist by reading), approach, tests, and verification. Specifically:

1. Ordering/grouping of the Week-7 in-repo fixes (#92, #168, #17, #382, #396-triage, #397, real-vault test, spans) into sensible PRs. Read the relevant files to pin down the concrete change for each: .github/workflows/deploy.yml (for #382/#397), frontend auth module (oidc-client-ts silent renew — find where the UserManager is configured), backend/app/api/v1/admin.py + frontend Admin page (for #92), backend/app/core/logging.py + settings (spans — recommend the OTel-neutral option: azure-monitor-opentelemetry vs opencensus status quo; keep it small).
2. A Playwright E2E expansion plan: which new specs to add (schedules, trigger bindings, notification config, run detail, check-editor variants), and how to structure an optional "live smoke" Playwright project/tag that runs against the deployed app + harness data (env-driven baseURL + real OIDC vs dev-bypass — recommend the simplest viable approach, e.g. storageState via manual login or keep live smoke as an API-level script + manual checklist). Keep CI green — live specs must not run in CI.
3. Docs enrichment structure: exact new/updated pages in docs/ + mkdocs.yml nav changes + README updates. Keep it MkDocs-strict-safe.
4. Where to record the MCP candidate list (recommend: context/post-v1-roadmap.md addition + a GitHub issue), with a proposed tiering (tier 1 high-value safe reads, tier 2 mutating with edit-permission, excluded list + rationale).
5. A final verification section: local gates (black/ruff/mypy/bandit/pytest, pnpm lint/test/e2e), then live verification steps against the deployed app + harness.

Return the complete plan in markdown. Be concrete about file paths (verify they exist), but keep it scannable — no code dumps, just precise change descriptions.

## Phase 2 — PR: `test(secrets): cover the AzureKeyVaultStore lazy-import branch` (A4)

**Goal:** Exercise `secrets.py:97–109` for real — the `from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential` / `SecretClient(vault_url=…, credential=…)` construction path, currently bypassed by every existing test (`monkeypatch.setattr(store, "_client_lazy", …)`).

**Files:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/tests/core/test_secrets.py` only.

**Approach:** `azure-identity`/`azure-keyvault-secrets` are already prod deps, so no fakes in `sys.modules` needed — `monkeypatch.setattr` the real `azure.identity.DefaultAzureCredential` and `azure.keyvault.secrets.SecretClient` with recording stubs, then call `store._client_lazy()` (or `get`/`set` end-to-end). Assert: (1) `SecretClient` constructed with the vault URL + credential instance, (2) second call returns the cached client (no second construction), (3) `get_secret`/`set_secret` reached through the real branch. Note in a comment this discharges the WEEK7 A4 tail of the closed #169.

**Verification:** `pytest --cov` shows `secrets.py` lazy-import lines covered; no network calls (stubs).

## Phase 3 — PR: `feat(observability): OTel request/task spans, Azure-exported` (A3)

**Goal:** Request + Celery-task spans to App Insights, OTel-neutral core (ADR 0010 seam), gated on the existing `applicationinsights_connection_string` setting (`backend/app/core/config.py:31`) — conn string unset → complete no-op, matching the logging pattern.

**Recommendation:** use `opentelemetry-sdk` + `opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi` + `opentelemetry-instrumentation-celery` + **`azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter`** (exporter only) — NOT the `azure-monitor-opentelemetry` distro. Rationale: the distro auto-configures logging/metrics and would collide with the carefully hardened structlog + opencensus `AzureLogHandler` chain in `logging.py` (the #393/#405 Py3.13 lock workaround); exporter-only keeps the PR one functionality and the core vendor-neutral (swap exporter per cloud, per ADR 0028 direction). Leave the opencensus *log* handler untouched; file a follow-up issue "migrate logs opencensus→OTel" (the fix already promised in the `logging.py:153` comment — opencensus is EOL).

**Files:**
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/requirements.txt` — the four pinned deps (next to line 40's `opencensus-ext-azure`).
- New `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/core/tracing.py` — `configure_tracing()`: build `TracerProvider` + `BatchSpanProcessor(AzureMonitorTraceExporter(connection_string=…))` when the setting is present; lazy-import all OTel/Azure bits (repo convention, see `secrets.py`).
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/main.py` — call `configure_tracing()` in the lifespan next to `configure_logging()` (line 43); `FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument_app(app)`, excluding `/healthz` and the webhook path (its `?token=` query must never land in span attributes — same concern as the #494 uvicorn.access silencing in `logging.py:121–131`).
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/worker/celery_app.py` — `CeleryInstrumentor().instrument()` via the `worker_process_init` signal (required: Celery forks).
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/pyproject.toml` — mypy `ignore_missing_imports` overrides for `opentelemetry.*` / `azure.monitor.*` if needed (pattern exists at line 64 for `opencensus.*`).
- New `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/tests/core/test_tracing.py` — conn string unset → no provider configured / app boots unchanged; conn string set + exporter stubbed → provider installed, span emitted on a test request (in-memory exporter), webhook/healthz excluded.

**Verification quick-ref (matches WEEK7 A3):** conn string unset → boots unchanged; mocked → spans emit. `pip-audit` clean after the dep add. Slow-query hook (`opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy`) deliberately deferred to the same follow-up issue — keeps this PR small.

## Phase 6 — PR: `test(e2e): opt-in live-smoke lane (never in CI)`

**Recommendation — keep browser-level live smoke minimal; lead with the API script.** Key constraint discovered: `authClient.ts` stores the OIDC user in **sessionStorage** (`WebStorageStateStore({ store: window.sessionStorage })`, line 59) — Playwright `storageState` captures cookies + localStorage only, so the "manual login → save storageState" trick does **not** work here without extra machinery.

**Approach:**
1. **Primary live gate = API-level:** `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` (exists, 12/12) parameterized with `--base-url` + a real bearer token against the deployed API — wire the harness suites (`DataQ-harness/dataq_setup/setup.py` flows) into a documented run order. No repo-CI coupling.
2. **Thin browser live-smoke (read-only), opt-in:** in `frontend/playwright.config.ts`, add a second project `live-smoke` with `testDir: './e2e-live'`, included **only when `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` is set** (spread-conditional in `projects`; no `webServer` for it). Auth: a `global-setup` that launches **headed**, navigates to the live URL, waits for a human to complete the real OIDC login (MFA), then serializes `sessionStorage` to a git-ignored JSON; specs inject it via `context.addInitScript`. 2–3 read-only specs: dashboard loads with harness data, suites list shows `flow_a_snowflake` etc., a run detail opens. CI never sets `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` → CI unchanged.
3. Manual checklist for the rest (trigger a live run, Teams alert arrives) in `frontend/e2e/README.md` + `docs/runbook-faq.md`.

**Files:** `frontend/playwright.config.ts`, new `frontend/e2e-live/` (specs + `global-setup.ts`), `frontend/.gitignore` (session-state JSON), `frontend/e2e/README.md`.

**Proposed tiering** (all as thin service-layer wrappers + `require_permission` + redaction, per the existing 8 in `backend/app/mcp/server.py`):
- **Tier 1 — high-value safe reads:** `list_checks`/`get_check` + check history; `list_runs` (filterable); `get_run` detail with per-check results (redacted); `list_connections` (names/types only, never config secrets); `list_schedules`; `list_trigger_bindings`; `get_notification_config`; `get_suite_performance` (dashboard endpoint); `export_suite` (redacted).
- **Tier 2 — mutating, gated on edit permission:** `update_check`/`delete_check`; `dryrun_check`; `snooze_check`; `cancel_run`; `create_schedule`/`delete_schedule`; `create_trigger_binding`; `import_suite`; `suggest_column_policy`.
- **Excluded + rationale:** connection create/update/reauth (credentials transiting an LLM — hard no); inbound webhooks (M2M surface); admin endpoints (workspace-admin scope, pending #488 design); probe/health internals; share mutations (permission escalation through a conversational interface).

## Suggested ordering

Phases 1→2 (quick closes, independent) → 3 (dep add; do before Phase-6 `pip-audit` gate matters) → 4→5 (E2E, sequential to keep CI churn low) → 6 → 7 → 8. Phases 7 and 8 are independent of everything and can interleave. Each PR: conventional-commit title, `Fixes #N` where applicable, Week-7/post-v1 milestone, `/code-review` before merge, squash-merge.

- New `frontend/e2e/schedules.spec.ts` — on the seeded "Orders quality" suite, drive `frontend/src/components/suites/SchedulesPanel.tsx`: create a cron schedule, assert it renders (next-run/timezone), invalid-cron validation message, delete.
- New `frontend/e2e/trigger-bindings.spec.ts` — drive `frontend/src/components/suites/TriggersPanel.tsx`: bind a pipeline, assert listed, unbind.
- New `frontend/e2e/notifications.spec.ts` — drive `frontend/src/components/suites/NotificationsPanel.tsx`: configure a channel + severity routing, assert persisted after reload.
- `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/scripts/demo_data.py` — extend the seed only where UI creation isn't possible: trigger bindings need an orchestration connection (demo_data currently seeds neither schedules nor notifications — grep confirms); seed one ADF-type connection + pipeline rows so the panel has something to bind. Follow the existing seed idempotency style.
- `frontend/e2e/README.md` — note the new specs + any seed additions.

**Approach notes:** reuse the antd interaction patterns already proven in `suites.spec.ts` (keyboard-select on `role=combobox`, dialog-anchored confirms, `Date.now()`-suffixed names, self-cleanup).

**Verification:** `pnpm e2e` locally against compose + seed; CI `frontend-e2e` stays green (workers:1, retries:2 already configured).

## Phase 5 — PR: `test(e2e): run-detail, dashboard, check-editor variants, admin`

**Files:**
- New `frontend/e2e/run-detail.spec.ts` — from `/results`, open a seeded run → `/results/:runId` (`frontend/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx`): per-check outcomes, redacted-sample panel, status chips.
- New `frontend/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` — `/dashboard` widgets render with seeded data (KPI cards, recent runs via `components/dashboard/RecentRuns.tsx`).
- Extend `frontend/e2e/suites.spec.ts` (or new `check-editor.spec.ts`) — the wizard's other categories (the category step already shows "Freshness" per the existing spec, line 35): author a custom-SQL check, a freshness monitor, a volume monitor; assert each lands on the suite detail. Delete after.
- New `frontend/e2e/admin.spec.ts` — `/admin` (`frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx`): suites/users/access tables + the webhook-config surface (#493). Prereq: the dev-bypass identity must be in the workspace-admin allowlist in CI — check the `frontend-e2e` job's backend env in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and add the admin allowlist setting if absent; if the bypass user can't be made admin cheaply, assert the 403 error page instead (still a real path).

**Verification:** same as Phase 4.

## Phase 6 — PR: `test(e2e): opt-in live-smoke lane (never in CI)`

**Recommendation — keep browser-level live smoke minimal; lead with the API script.** Key constraint discovered: `authClient.ts` stores the OIDC user in **sessionStorage** (`WebStorageStateStore({ store: window.sessionStorage })`, line 59) — Playwright `storageState` captures cookies + localStorage only, so the "manual login → save storageState" trick does **not** work here without extra machinery.

**Approach:**
1. **Primary live gate = API-level:** `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` (exists, 12/12) parameterized with `--base-url` + a real bearer token against the deployed API — wire the harness suites (`DataQ-harness/dataq_setup/setup.py` flows) into a documented run order. No repo-CI coupling.
2. **Thin browser live-smoke (read-only), opt-in:** in `frontend/playwright.config.ts`, add a second project `live-smoke` with `testDir: './e2e-live'`, included **only when `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` is set** (spread-conditional in `projects`; no `webServer` for it). Auth: a `global-setup` that launches **headed**, navigates to the live URL, waits for a human to complete the real OIDC login (MFA), then serializes `sessionStorage` to a git-ignored JSON; specs inject it via `context.addInitScript`. 2–3 read-only specs: dashboard loads with harness data, suites list shows `flow_a_snowflake` etc., a run detail opens. CI never sets `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` → CI unchanged.
3. Manual checklist for the rest (trigger a live run, Teams alert arrives) in `frontend/e2e/README.md` + `docs/runbook-faq.md`.

**Files:** `frontend/playwright.config.ts`, new `frontend/e2e-live/` (specs + `global-setup.ts`), `frontend/.gitignore` (session-state JSON), `frontend/e2e/README.md`.

## Phase 7 — PR(s): docs enrichment (file a fresh issue first — #288 is closed; reference it as the parent)

Split into two PRs if large: (a) new pages, (b) enrichment of existing pages. All MkDocs-strict-safe: every new page added to `mkdocs.yml` nav, relative links only, no orphaned pages (note `validation.omitted_files: ignore` is set, but nav completeness is still the convention).

**New pages in `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/docs/`:**
- `notifications.md` — Teams/Slack/email channel setup, per-suite config (the NotificationsPanel walkthrough), severity routing, dedup, snooze/suppression, always-alert-on-operational-failure semantics (#413/#419/#369/#370 features — currently undocumented on the site; grep shows zero user-facing notification docs).
- `scheduling.md` — cron syntax, timezone/DST behavior, no-backfill semantics, gap recovery, stuck-run reaper (C2).
- `best-practices.md` — suite/check organization, severity assignment (ADR 0005/0016), redaction posture, when to use expectation vs custom-SQL vs freshness/volume.
- `feature-matrix.md` — check kinds × datasources grid (Snowflake / UC / flat-file; note #520: freshness/volume are SQL-only), modes, orchestration providers.

**Updated pages:**
- `datasources-checks.md` — per-check-type authoring walkthroughs (expectation wizard, custom SQL per ADR 0019, freshness/volume per ADR 0012) keyed to the real routes (`/suites/:id/checks/new`).
- `observability.md` — deepen the "Common failures" table; add the spans/App-Insights view once Phase 3 lands.
- `orchestration.md` — cross-link the Admin webhook surface (#493) as the way to get the ADF webhook URL.
- `mkdocs.yml` — nav: consider grouping into sections now that pages ≥13, e.g. `Guides:` (getting-started, datasources-checks, scheduling, notifications, orchestration, best-practices) / `Reference:` (feature-matrix, architecture, observability, runbook-faq, glossary, contributing).
- `README.md` — add the new page links under `## Documentation` (line 67).

**Verification:** `mkdocs build --strict` locally; docs.yml publishes on merge; click-through the live site nav.

## Phase 8 — PR: `docs(roadmap): MCP tool-expansion candidate list` + GitHub issue (analysis only)

**Where:** new **"Theme 13 — MCP tool expansion"** in `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/context/post-v1-roadmap.md` (themes 1–12 exist; add before "How this maps to GitHub") + one `gh issue create` (enhancement, post-v1 milestone) mirroring it, cross-linking ADR 0008 and open #488 (workspace-admin MCP visibility) and #461/ADR 0026 (API-key auth for headless MCP).

**Proposed tiering** (all as thin service-layer wrappers + `require_permission` + redaction, per the existing 8 in `backend/app/mcp/server.py`):
- **Tier 1 — high-value safe reads:** `list_checks`/`get_check` + check history; `list_runs` (filterable); `get_run` detail with per-check results (redacted); `list_connections` (names/types only, never config secrets); `list_schedules`; `list_trigger_bindings`; `get_notification_config`; `get_suite_performance` (dashboard endpoint); `export_suite` (redacted).
- **Tier 2 — mutating, gated on edit permission:** `update_check`/`delete_check`; `dryrun_check`; `snooze_check`; `cancel_run`; `create_schedule`/`delete_schedule`; `create_trigger_binding`; `import_suite`; `suggest_column_policy`.
- **Excluded + rationale:** connection create/update/reauth (credentials transiting an LLM — hard no); inbound webhooks (M2M surface); admin endpoints (workspace-admin scope, pending #488 design); probe/health internals; share mutations (permission escalation through a conversational interface).

## External / live items (not PRs — coordination checklist)

1. **#492 Azure Monitor→ADF alert rule** (the headline Track-A remainder): action groups + alert rules on pre-prod/prod factories → deliberate-failure smoke, DataQ UI shows the failure <5 min. The webhook URL now comes from the Admin page (#493) — no hand-assembly.
2. **Live harness smoke:** run `DataQ-harness/dataq_setup/setup.py` flows; verify DataQ-side check runs green on Flows A/B/C; then flip the 🟡 deferred-smoke rows in `docs/progress.md` + CLAUDE.md §13 (a small `docs(progress)` PR).
3. **Claude Desktop MCP E2E** (B4 tail): 4 canonical NL queries against the live `/mcp` — validates the `JWTVerifier` audience assumption.
4. **KV purge-protection tfvars** — external, irreversible; record the decision either way in `deploy/README.md`.
5. **D4 team onboarding** — process, not code.

## Suggested ordering

Phases 1→2 (quick closes, independent) → 3 (dep add; do before Phase-6 `pip-audit` gate matters) → 4→5 (E2E, sequential to keep CI churn low) → 6 → 7 → 8. Phases 7 and 8 are independent of everything and can interleave. Each PR: conventional-commit title, `Fixes #N` where applicable, Week-7/post-v1 milestone, `/code-review` before merge, squash-merge.

## Final verification gates

**Local, before every push (working agreement):**
- `black --check` · `ruff check` · `mypy` (incl. `backend/tests`, #418) · `bandit` · `pytest --cov` (hold ~91%)

2. Phase-6 live-smoke lane (or manual checklist) vs `https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io` with harness data.
3. Spans visible in App Insights (request + task traces correlated by request_id) after Phase 3.
4. #492 deliberate-failure alert <5 min; Claude Desktop 4-query MCP smoke; then close the loop in `docs/progress.md`.

- /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/core/logging.py (spans wiring pattern + the opencensus constraint Phase 3 must not disturb)

# DataQ — Week 7 working tracker (Deployment, hardening & docs)

> **NOT git-tracked** (matched by `.gitignore` → `*_TODO.md`, same as `HARNESS_TODO.md`).
> Local working checklist for Week 7. Source of truth for task ticks stays
> [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md); this is the day-to-day scratch tracker.
>
> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[-]` deferred/skipped · 🔒 blocked

---

## Context / sequencing

> **STATUS 2026-06-29 — TRACK A done + TRACKS B/C/D/E largely DONE.** DataQ v1 is LIVE in
> Azure (image `:v10`). This session landed: **FastMCP server** (Track B, #460), **consistency
> hardening** C1/C2 (#456/#458), **hardening/docs** D1/D2/D3 + A2 prod-docs gate (#465/#468/#464),
> **prototype polish** E1/E2 (#459 + verified), and the **W1–6 deferred + not-started triages**
> (#463/#467) + ADR 0026 stub (#462, #461). Filed #457/#466. **What's left = external/coordination:**
> Azure Monitor→ADF webhook, team onboarding (D4), live smoke, + small in-repo tails (A3 spans, A4
> vault test, A5 token refresh). C1/C2/C3 consistency hardening all done (#456/#458/#470).
> See `docs/progress.md` Week 7 (31/40).

- **Harness is live end-to-end** (`HARNESS_TODO.md`, 2026-06-28): ADF + Airflow load
  Snowflake + UC + flat files (incl. true Event-Grid push); mockdata cron Jobs seeded. The
  one gate that remained — **DataQ's own cloud deploy** so its poller/check-runs read that data
  — **has cleared.** 5 live prod connections + a "Retail Orders" demo suite are configured;
  **verified DataQ-side check-runs against the harness are the last step to discharge the
  deferred live warehouse/file smoke** (in progress).
- **Cloud apply DONE** (the RP-registration / Owner blocker is resolved): the Contributor SP
  self-registered the RPs. ACA (`dataq-app-{api,worker,migrate}`) + SWA (`dataq-app-web`,
  same-origin `/api`) + Redis + Key Vault (UAMI) + App Insights live on the shared `dataq-cae`
  env; distinct `dataq` DB + `dataq_app` role on the shared Postgres. ADRs 0023/0024/0025.
- **Out-of-band since deploy:** alerting expansion (Slack+email #413, always-alert #419, race
  #420), column-aware redaction (#417), per-run outcome in runs table (#425), mypy-on-tests
  (#418), and **freshness/volume monitor-kinds end-to-end** (#426/#437 — ADR 0012 Theme A
  pulled forward). Plus post-v1 design docs (#422/#430/#436) → now consolidated in
  `context/post-v1-roadmap.md`.
- Harness issue-filing **out of scope**.

### What #379 already shipped — DON'T rebuild
- [x] `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf`
- [x] `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (`workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` + `frontend` jobs)
- [x] `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example`
- [x] `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` (coded, lazy-import, **0% cov**)
- [x] `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` opencensus `AzureLogHandler` (conn-string-gated)
- [x] CORS middleware in `main.py`; `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`

---

## Track A (LEAD) — Deployment — ✅ DONE (DataQ live in Azure, image `:v10`)

### Phase 1 — deploy-ready hardening
- [x] **A0 — container registry → GHCR + ADR 0023** — ADR written + indexed; `deploy.yml` logs in to
      `ghcr.io` via `GITHUB_TOKEN`, pushes `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:<tag>`; package public →
      ACA pulls anonymously, no stored registry credential. (#392 / #394)
- [x] **A1 — CI/CD safety gate (`deploy.yml`)** — migrate-gate on app rollout (#381); migrate/pnpm bugs
      fixed (#403, closes #401/#402); validated end-to-end (build→push→`alembic upgrade head`→ACA roll→SWA).
      _Remaining nit: `frontend needs: backend` + SHA-pin before enabling `push:[main]` — [#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382) (open, W7)._
- [x] **A2 — prod-docs gate** · [#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170) — `docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url = None` when `environment == "prod"`; pure `docs_kwargs` helper + tests — **#464 (closes #170)**
- [ ] 🟡 **A3 — App Insights integration** — **logs/exceptions live** (conn-string-gated `AzureLogHandler`
      on api+worker; #393/#405/#406 hardened it on Py3.13). **Pending:** request/task span instrumentation
